summary: Linked the Master Hand laser item unit by adding its missing ItemStateTable data symbol and flipping src/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The decomp.dev report confirmed one new match for main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser .data, bringing that unit's .data from 0.00% to 100.00% and adding 16 bytes of matched data.
what_changed: Changed the configure.py object entry for melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
The added item state table contains one state with state id 0 and callbacks itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Anim, itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itMasterhandlaser_UnkMotion0_Coll.
No PR body or human review comments were present in the provided slice; the only comment was the decomp.dev automated match report.
smart_moves: Matched the unit by supplying the missing data object rather than altering existing code flow.
Used the existing item callback names already present in the source for the ItemStateTable entries, keeping the new data declaration minimal and directly tied to known functions.
Only promoted the object to Matching after adding the data needed for the unit-level match, making the configure.py change evidence-backed by the bot report.
decomp_lessons: For item units that are functionally decompiled but remain NonMatching, check for missing ItemStateTable data before chasing codegen issues.
A single missing state table can block a unit's .data match even when the callback functions already exist.
When an item has only one state, the ItemStateTable can be represented as a one-element array with the state id and Anim/Phys/Coll function pointers.
After adding required data symbols, remember to update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching so the build system links the source object as matched.
naming_conventions: Raw data symbol retained the address-style project name it_803F9378.
Master Hand laser callbacks use the mixed-case item prefix itMasterhandlaser_ followed by motion labels such as UnkMotion0_Anim, UnkMotion0_Phys, and UnkMotion0_Coll.
The source file follows the item path convention src/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c and configure.py uses the corresponding object path melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Resolved a .data mismatch by reconstructing the ItemStateTable layout with the correct callback pointer order: anim, phys, coll.
Promoted the object in configure.py from NonMatching to Matching only after the data symbol addition produced a complete match.
The automated report measured the effect as +16 bytes of matched .data and +56 bytes of linked data.
review_feedback: No human review feedback was present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev reported one new match: main/melee/it/items/itmasterhandlaser .data improved from 0.00% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other item source files still marked Object(NonMatching) in configure.py with missing ItemStateTable declarations.
Search for address-style item data symbols near it_803F9378 to identify adjacent item state tables.
Compare itmasterhandlaser.c against itmasterhandbullet.c and itcrazyhandbomb.c for similar item state table patterns.
